heading: Special valuation rules in case of transfers of certain interests in corporations or partnerships
label: 2701 Special valuation rules in case of transfers of certain interests in corporations or partnerships

L. 104–188Pub. L. 101–508, title XIsection 1702(i) of Pub. L. 104–188section 38 of this title

Effective Date, , , provided that:Pub. L. 101–508, title XI, § 11602(e)(1)104 Stat. 1388–500Nov. 5, 1990“(A).—In generalThe amendments made by subsection (a) [enacting this chapter]—“(i)to the extent such amendments relate to sections 2701 and 2702 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 (as added by such amendments), shall apply to transfers after ,October 8, 1990“(ii)to the extent such amendments relate to section 2703 of such Code (as so added), shall apply to—“(I)agreements, options, rights, or restrictions entered into or granted after , andOctober 8, 1990“(II)agreements, options, rights, or restrictions which are substantially modified after , andOctober 8, 1990“(iii)to the extent such amendments relate to section 2704 of such Code (as so added), shall apply to restrictions or rights (or limitations on rights) created after .October 8, 1990“(B).—ExceptionFor purposes of subparagraph (A)(i), with respect to property transferred before —October 9, 1990“(i)any failure to exercise a right of conversion,“(ii)any failure to pay dividends, and“(iii)any failure to exercise other rights specified in regulations,shall not be treated as a subsequent transfer.”

Time for Election Under Subsection (c)(3)(C)(i), , , provided that:Pub. L. 104–188, title I, § 1702(f)(5)(C)110 Stat. 1871Aug. 20, 1996“The time for making an election under the second sentence of section 2701(c)(3)(C)(i) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 (as amended by subparagraph (A)) shall not expire before the due date (including extensions) for filing the transferor’s return of the tax imposed by section 2501 of such Code for the first calendar year ending after the date of enactment [probably means the date of enactment of , ].”Pub. Study of Methods Used To Distort Valuation of Property for Purposes of Estate and Gift Tax, , , directed the Secretary of the Treasury to conduct a study of the prevalence and types of options and agreements used to distort the valuation of property for purposes of subtitle B of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, and other methods using discretionary rights to distort this valuation, and report to Congress the results of the study, together with any legislative recommendations, not later than .Pub. L. 101–508, title XI, § 11602(d)104 Stat. 1388–500Nov. 5, 1990Dec. 31, 1992

children.label: A
children.text: which is described in subparagraph (A) or (B) of subsection (b)(1), and

children.label: B
children.text: which is with respect to any applicable retained interest that is held by the transferor or an applicable family member immediately after the transfer,

children.heading: Exceptions for marketable retained interests, etc.
children.label: 2 Exceptions for marketable retained interests, etc.
A
children.text: market quotations are readily available (as of the date of the transfer) for such interest on an established securities market,

children.label: B
children.text: such interest is of the same class as the transferred interest, or

children.label: C
children.text: such interest is proportionally the same as the transferred interest, without regard to nonlapsing differences in voting power (or, for a partnership, nonlapsing differences with respect to management and limitations on liability).

children.heading: Valuation of rights to which paragraph (1) applies
children.label: 3 Valuation of rights to which paragraph (1) applies

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The value of any right described in paragraph (1), other than a distribution right which consists of a right to receive a qualified payment, shall be treated as being zero.

children.heading: Valuation of certain qualified payments
children.label: B Valuation of certain qualified payments
i
children.text: any applicable retained interest confers a distribution right which consists of the right to a qualified payment, and

children.label: ii
children.text: there are 1 or more liquidation, put, call, or conversion rights with respect to such interest,

children.heading: Valuation of qualified payments where no liquidation, etc. rights
children.label: C Valuation of qualified payments where no liquidation, etc. rights
children.text: In the case of an applicable retained interest which is described in subparagraph (B)(i) but not subparagraph (B)(ii), the value of the distribution right shall be determined without regard to this section.

children.label: i
children.text: the total value of all of the equity interests in such entity, plus

children.label: ii
children.text: the total amount of indebtedness of such entity to the transferor (or an applicable family member).

children.heading: Junior equity interest
children.label: i Junior equity interest
children.text: The term “junior equity interest” means common stock or, in the case of a partnership, any partnership interest under which the rights as to income and capital (or, to the extent provided in regulations, the rights as to either income or capital) are junior to the rights of all other classes of equity interests.

children.heading: Equity interest
children.label: ii Equity interest
children.text: The term “equity interest” means stock or any interest as a partner, as the case may be.

children.label: A
children.text: a distribution right, but only if, immediately before the transfer described in subsection (a)(1), the transferor and applicable family members hold (after application of subsection (e)(3)) control of the entity, or

children.label: B
children.text: a liquidation, put, call, or conversion right.

children.heading: Corporations
children.label: A Corporations
children.text: In the case of a corporation, the term “control” means the holding of at least 50 percent (by vote or value) of the stock of the corporation.

children.label: i
children.text: the holding of at least 50 percent of the capital or profits interests in the partnership, or

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of a limited partnership, the holding of any interest as a general partner.

children.heading: Applicable family member
children.label: C Applicable family member
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “applicable family member” includes any lineal descendant of any parent of the transferor or the transferor’s spouse.

children.heading: Distribution and other rights; qualified payments
children.label: c Distribution and other rights; qualified payments
i
children.text: a right to distributions from a corporation with respect to its stock, and

children.label: ii
children.text: a right to distributions from a partnership with respect to a partner’s interest in the partnership.

children.label: i
children.text: a right to distributions with respect to any interest which is junior to the rights of the transferred interest,

children.label: ii
children.text: any liquidation, put, call, or conversion right, or

children.label: iii
children.text: any right to receive any guaranteed payment described in section 707(c) of a fixed amount.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “liquidation, put, call, or conversion right” means any liquidation, put, call, or conversion right, or any similar right, the exercise or nonexercise of which affects the value of the transferred interest.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The term “liquidation, put, call, or conversion right” does not include any right which must be exercised at a specific time and at a specific amount.

children.heading: Treatment of certain rights
children.label: ii Treatment of certain rights
children.text: If a right is assumed to be exercised in a particular manner under subsection (a)(3)(B), such right shall be treated as so exercised for purposes of clause (i).

children.heading: Exception for certain rights to convert
children.label: C Exception for certain rights to convert
i
children.text: is a right to convert into a fixed number (or a fixed percentage) of shares of the same class of stock in a corporation as the transferred stock in such corporation under subsection (a)(1) (or stock which would be of the same class but for nonlapsing differences in voting power),

children.label: ii
children.text: is nonlapsing,

children.label: iii
children.text: is subject to proportionate adjustments for splits, combinations, reclassifications, and similar changes in the capital stock, and

children.label: iv
children.text: is subject to adjustments similar to the adjustments under subsection (d) for accumulated but unpaid distributions.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in this paragraph, the term “qualified payment” means any dividend payable on a periodic basis under any cumulative preferred stock (or a comparable payment under any partnership interest) to the extent that such dividend (or comparable payment) is determined at a fixed rate.

children.heading: Treatment of variable rate payments
children.label: B Treatment of variable rate payments
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), a payment shall be treated as fixed as to rate if such payment is determined at a rate which bears a fixed relationship to a specified market interest rate.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Payments under any interest held by a transferor which (without regard to this subparagraph) are qualified payments shall be treated as qualified payments unless the transferor elects not to treat such payments as qualified payments. Payments described in the preceding sentence which are held by an applicable family member shall be treated as qualified payments only if such member elects to treat such payments as qualified payments.

children.heading: Election to have interest treated as qualified payment
children.label: ii Election to have interest treated as qualified payment
children.text: A transferor or applicable family member holding any distribution right which (without regard to this subparagraph) is not a qualified payment may elect to treat such right as a qualified payment, to be paid in the amounts and at the times specified in such election. The preceding sentence shall apply only to the extent that the amounts and times so specified are not inconsistent with the underlying legal instrument giving rise to such right.

children.heading: Elections irrevocable
children.label: iii Elections irrevocable
children.text: Any election under this subparagraph with respect to an interest shall, once made, be irrevocable.

children.heading: Transfer tax treatment of cumulative but unpaid distributions
children.label: d Transfer tax treatment of cumulative but unpaid distributions
A
children.text: The taxable estate of the transferor in the case of a taxable event described in paragraph (3)(A)(i).

children.label: B
children.text: The taxable gifts of the transferor for the calendar year in which the taxable event occurs in the case of a taxable event described in paragraph (3)(A)(ii) or (iii).

children.label: I
children.text: all such payments were paid on the date payment was due, and

children.label: II
children.text: all such payments were reinvested by the transferor as of the date of payment at a yield equal to the discount rate used in determining the value of the applicable retained interest described in subsection (a)(1), over

children.label: ii
children.text: the value of such payments paid during such period computed under clause (i) on the basis of the time when such payments were actually paid.

children.heading: Limitation on amount of increase
children.label: B Limitation on amount of increase
I
children.text: the value (determined as of the date of the taxable event) of all equity interests in the entity which are junior to the applicable retained interest, over

children.label: II
children.text: the value of such interests (determined as of the date of the transfer to which subsection (a)(1) applied).

children.label: I
children.text: the number of shares in the corporation held (as of the date of the taxable event) by the transferor which are applicable retained interests of the same class, by

children.label: II
children.text: the total number of shares in such corporation (as of such date) which are of the same class as the class described in subclause (I).

children.heading: Definition
children.label: iii Definition
children.text: For purposes of this subparagraph, the term “equity interest” has the meaning given such term by subsection (a)(4)(B).

children.heading: Grace period
children.label: C Grace period
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), any payment of any distribution during the 4-year period beginning on its due date shall be treated as having been made on such due date.

children.label: i
children.text: The death of the transferor if the applicable retained interest conferring the distribution right is includible in the estate of the transferor.

children.label: ii
children.text: The transfer of such applicable retained interest.

children.label: iii
children.text: At the election of the taxpayer, the payment of any qualified payment after the period described in paragraph (2)(C), but only with respect to such payment.

children.heading: Exception where spouse is transferee
children.label: B Exception where spouse is transferee

children.heading: Deathtime transfers
children.label: i Deathtime transfers
children.text: Subparagraph (A)(i) shall not apply to any interest includible in the gross estate of the transferor if a deduction with respect to such interest is allowable under section 2056 or 2106(a)(3).

children.label: I
children.text: any deduction allowed under section 2523, or the exclusion under section 2503(b), or

children.label: II
children.text: consideration for the transfer provided by the spouse.

children.heading: Spouse succeeds to treatment of transferor
children.label: iii Spouse succeeds to treatment of transferor
children.text: If an event is not treated as a taxable event by reason of this subparagraph, the transferee spouse or surviving spouse (as the case may be) shall be treated in the same manner as the transferor in applying this subsection with respect to the interest involved.

children.heading: Special rules for applicable family members
children.label: 4 Special rules for applicable family members

children.heading: Family member treated in same manner as transferor
children.label: A Family member treated in same manner as transferor
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, an applicable family member shall be treated in the same manner as the transferor with respect to any distribution right retained by such family member to which subsection (a)(3)(B) or (C) applied.

children.heading: Transfer to applicable family member
children.label: B Transfer to applicable family member
children.text: In the case of a taxable event described in paragraph (3)(A)(ii) involving the transfer of an applicable retained interest to an applicable family member (other than the spouse of the transferor), the applicable family member shall be treated in the same manner as the transferor in applying this subsection to distributions accumulating with respect to such interest after such taxable event.

children.heading: Transfer to transferors
children.label: C Transfer to transferors
children.text: In the case of a taxable event described in paragraph (3)(A)(ii) involving a transfer of an applicable retained interest from an applicable family member to a transferor, this subsection shall continue to apply to the transferor during any period the transferor holds such interest.

children.heading: Transfer to include termination
children.label: 5 Transfer to include termination
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, any termination of an interest shall be treated as a transfer.

children.label: A
children.text: the transferor’s spouse,

children.label: B
children.text: a lineal descendant of the transferor or the transferor’s spouse, and

children.label: C
children.text: the spouse of any such descendant.

children.label: A
children.text: the transferor’s spouse,

children.label: B
children.text: an ancestor of the transferor or the transferor’s spouse, and

children.label: C
children.text: the spouse of any such ancestor.

children.heading: Attribution of indirect holdings and transfers
children.label: 3 Attribution of indirect holdings and transfers
children.text: An individual shall be treated as holding any interest to the extent such interest is held indirectly by such individual through a corporation, partnership, trust, or other entity. If any individual is treated as holding any interest by reason of the preceding sentence, any transfer which results in such interest being treated as no longer held by such individual shall be treated as a transfer of such interest.

children.heading: Effect of adoption
children.label: 4 Effect of adoption
children.text: A relationship by legal adoption shall be treated as a relationship by blood.

children.label: A
children.text: receives an applicable retained interest in such entity pursuant to such transaction, or

children.label: B
children.text: under regulations, otherwise holds, immediately after such transaction, an applicable retained interest in such entity.

children.heading: Adjustments
children.label: 6 Adjustments
children.text: Under regulations prescribed by the Secretary, if there is any subsequent transfer, or inclusion in the gross estate, of any applicable retained interest which was valued under the rules of subsection (a), appropriate adjustments shall be made for purposes of chapter 11, 12, or 13 to reflect the increase in the amount of any prior taxable gift made by the transferor or decedent by reason of such valuation or to reflect the application of subsection (d).

children.heading: Treatment as separate interests
children.label: 7 Treatment as separate interests
children.text: The Secretary may by regulation provide that any applicable retained interest shall be treated as 2 or more separate interests for purposes of this section.
